entries.name: MagneticCurveOptionSetCommand
entries.title: Magnetic line options
entries.summary: This command allows you to set by default options of the [Magnetic Line](MagneticLineCreationCommand.md).

entries.name: MagneticLineCreationCommand
entries.title: Magnetic line
entries.summary: This command allows you to create lines where BOM indexes or some annotations can be positioned. By moving the line, the attached indexes and annotations will move too.

entries.name: BendingTextsGenerationCommand
entries.title: Automatic bend notes
entries.summary: This command allows you to generate automatically all the bending notes on a view that contains an unfolding.

entries.name: AutomaticBomRichTextCommand
entries.title: Automatic Bill of Material Index
entries.summary: This command allows you to automatically locate all parts of a view.

entries.name: BomInclusionCommand
entries.title: Inclusion bill of material table
entries.summary: This command allows a dimension table to the inserted in your draft document.

entries.name: BomIndexStyleCommand
entries.title: Index Style
entries.summary: This command allows you to create/modify index styles, which will then be used to create [BOM indexes](../Detailing/Bom/BomRichTextCommand.md).

entries.name: BomTableCommand
entries.title: Bill of material table
entries.summary: This command allows a dimension table to the inserted in your draft document.

entries.name: BookCommand
entries.title: This command activate a specific management of the document.
entries.summary: This command activate a specific management of the document.

entries.name: ChapterCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a chapter of the [book](BookCommand.md) that will be automatically filled according to its settings and the [paragraph model](Models/ParagraphModelCommand.md) used
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a chapter of the [book](BookCommand.md) that will be automatically filled according to its settings and the [paragraph model](Models/ParagraphModelCommand.md) used

entries.name: ParagraphModelCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a paragraph model that will be used by a [chapiter](../ChapterCommand.md) of a draft document in which the "[Book](../BookCommand.md)" has been activated.
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a paragraph model that will be used by a [chapiter](../ChapterCommand.md) of a draft document in which the "[Book](../BookCommand.md)" has been activated.

entries.name: SetPageCommand
entries.title: This command allows to customize the display of a view in a draft with several pages.
entries.summary: Possibility to combine multiple options.

entries.name: PropertyTableCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create inside a [book](../BookCommand.md), a table that displays properties.
entries.summary: This command allows to create inside a [book](../BookCommand.md), a table that displays properties.

entries.name: TextCommand
entries.title: The **Text** command from the draft allows to insert a text into a draft, a title block, the detailing of a view, a sketch...
entries.summary: The **Text** command from the draft allows to insert a text into a draft, a title block, the detailing of a view, a sketch...

entries.name: ModelViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows to insert a projected view into a [paragraph model](../Models/ParagraphModelCommand.md). This view can, for example, display the tool associated with a process or the state of the part .
entries.summary: This command allows to insert a projected view into a [paragraph model](../Models/ParagraphModelCommand.md). This view can, for example, display the tool associated with a process or the state of the part .

entries.name: AntiClockwiseQuarterTurnCommand
entries.title: This command allows to rotate aquarter turnin thecounterclockwise, the draft during its positioning in a bundle.
entries.summary: This command allows to rotate aquarter turnin thecounterclockwise, the draft during its positioning in a bundle.

entries.name: ClockwiseQuarterTurnCommand
entries.title: This command allows to rotate aquarter turnin theclockwise, the draft during its positioning in a bundle.
entries.summary: This command allows to rotate aquarter turnin theclockwise, the draft during its positioning in a bundle.

entries.name: UpdateTableOfContentsCommand
entries.title: This command allows to update folios of drafts included in a bundle.
entries.summary: This command allows to update folios of drafts included in a bundle.

entries.name: ShowAllConstraintsModeCommand
entries.title: Show constraints
entries.summary: This mode activates or deactivates the viewing of positioning constraints during the positioning edition of a draft.

entries.name: StartCrossSectionCommand
entries.title: Cross Section (Drafting Bundle)
entries.summary: This command allows to copy a cross section on another drafting of drafting bundle.

entries.name: StartDetailViewCommand
entries.title: Detail View (Drafting Bundle)
entries.summary: This command allows to copy a detail view on another drafting of drafting bundle.

entries.name: StartDirectionViewCommand
entries.title: View along a Direction
entries.summary: This command allows to copy a view along a direction on another drafting of drafting bundle.

entries.name: IncludeDraftingCommand
entries.title: Inclusion
entries.summary: This command allows to

entries.name: IncludeInLastPositioningModeCommand
entries.title: Inclusion in last positioning
entries.summary: The deactivated mode gives better performances when drafts are moved with the mouse.

entries.name: NestingDraftingsCommand
entries.title: This command allows to automatically position a selection of draftings
entries.summary: This command allows to automatically position a selection of draftings into a Bundle

entries.name: PaperCommand
entries.title: This document allows to define the border of a [draft bundle](../Documents/DraftingBundleDocumentWindowFactory.md). It will contain all draft documents to be able to print them.
entries.summary: This document allows to define the border of a [draft bundle](../Documents/DraftingBundleDocumentWindowFactory.md). It will contain all draft documents to be able to print them.

entries.name: AutomaticPositioningCommand
entries.title: Constraint
entries.summary: This command automatically constrains the draft to be included by recognition.

entries.name: AxisOnAxisConstraintCommand
entries.title: This command constrains an axis of the draft to be included in a [bundle](../Documents/DraftingBundleDocumentWindowFactory.md) in relation to an axis of the destination geometry.

entries.name: AxisOnPointConstraintCommand
entries.title: This command constrains an axis of the draft to be included in a [bundle](../Documents/DraftingBundleDocumentWindowFactory.md) in relation to a point of the destination geometry.

entries.name: DynamicPositioningModeCommand
entries.title: This mode activates or deactivates the dynamic positioning of drafts in a bundle of drafts.
entries.summary: To improve the response time when inserting drafts in the bundle of drafts including many constraints, it may be wise to deactivate this mode.

entries.name: OrientationCommand
entries.title: This command allows to orient the draft to be included in a [bundle](../Documents/DraftingBundleDocumentWindowFactory.md) in relation to a destination geometry.
entries.summary: - By default, during a draft inclusion in a bundle, the orientation constraint is automatically created.

entries.name: PointOnAxisConstraintCommand
entries.title: This command constrains a point of the draft to be included in a [bundle](../Documents/DraftingBundleDocumentWindowFactory.md) in relation to an axis of the destination geometry.

entries.name: BundleSketchCommand
entries.title: Sketch
entries.summary: This command allows to create a sketch in a [drafts bundle](../Documents/DraftingBundleDocumentWindowFactory.md). The sketch of a bundle always uses a scale factor to 1.

entries.name: DraftingDocumentCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create the draft of the selected document (Assembly, Part, Exploded, ...)
entries.summary: This command allows you to create the draft of the selected document (Assembly, Part, Exploded, ...)

entries.name: DraftingMultiplePrintCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to print all the draft document of a project or a folder (and its sub-folder).
entries.summary: This command allows you to print all the draft document of a project or a folder (and its sub-folder).

entries.name: InstanceDraftingDocumentCommand
entries.title: Predefined draft
entries.summary: This contextual command allows to use a predefined draft of a family instance included in an assembly.

entries.name: IsolateDraftingCommand
entries.title: Isolate drafting
entries.summary: - This operation is irreversible and the elements based on elements to isolate become basic. It will not be possible to reconnect broken links

entries.name: MirrorDraftingCommand
entries.title: Mirror drafting
entries.summary: This command allows to create a mirror drafting of a part or an assembly.

entries.name: PredefinedDraftCommand
entries.title: Predefined draft
entries.summary: This command allows to create a predefined draft from a [family](../../../Design/UI/Families/FamilyDocumentCommand.md), to be able to use this draft for each instance of this family.

entries.name: PrintCommand
entries.title: This command allows the current draft document to be printed.
entries.summary: This command allows the current draft document to be printed.

entries.name: ReplaceModelCommand
entries.title: Replace Model
entries.summary: This command allows to Replace a model (frame, title block,...) by another.

entries.name: CoordinatesTableCommand
entries.title: Coordinates Table
entries.summary: When the height is fixed, it can also be modified with a drag and drop of the upper separation of this line.

entries.name: CoordinatesTableIndexStyleCommand
entries.title: Coordinates Table Index Style
entries.summary: This command allows to create/ modify coordinates table index usable during [coordinates table](CoordinatesTableCommand.md) creation.

entries.name: CoordinatesTableStyleCommand
entries.title: Coordinates Table style
entries.summary: This command allows to create/modify [coordinates table](CoordinatesTableCommand.md) styles usable during coordinates tables creation.

entries.name: BendingTextCommand
entries.title: Bend note
entries.summary: This command allows to rapidly add a bend note by displaying all informations about the selected bend.

entries.name: BendingTextStyleCommand
entries.title: Bend note style
entries.summary: This command allows creating a customized bend note style.

entries.name: BomRichTextCommand
entries.title: Bill of material index
entries.summary: This command allows dimension index to be created in your draft document.

entries.name: BomRichTextStyleCommand
entries.title: Bom Index Style
entries.summary: This command allows you to create/modify index styles, which will then be used to create [BOM indexes](BomRichTextCommand.md).

entries.name: toolingbomrichtextscommand
entries.title: This command allows you to crate a bill of material index in your Draft document, like the [Bill of material index](../BomRichTextCommand.md). It allows moreover, to index automatically the same part in complementary views, like the [Automatic BOM Index](../../../Bom/AutomaticBomRichTextCommand.md).
entries.summary: This command allows you to crate a bill of material index in your Draft document, like the [Bill of material index](../BomRichTextCommand.md). It allows moreover, to index automatically the same part in complementary views, like the [Automatic BOM Index](../../../Bom/AutomaticBomRichTextCommand.md).

entries.name: ChamferTextCommand
entries.title: Chamfer Dimension
entries.summary: This command allows you to create chamfer dimensions.

entries.name: ChamferTextStyleCommand
entries.title: Chamfer Dimension Style
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a customized chamfer dimension style.

entries.name: DrillingRichTextCommand
entries.title: Drilling note
entries.summary: This command allows a note to be applied quickly to a drilling by indicating the information suitable for the selected geometry.

entries.name: DrillingRichTextStyleCommand
entries.title: Drilling note style
entries.summary: This command allows a customized drilling note style to be created.

entries.name: EdgeStateCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create an edge state note compatible with the
entries.summary: This command allows to create an edge state note compatible with the ISO 13715 standard.

entries.name: EdgeStateStyleCommand
entries.title: This command allows edge state styles to be created / modified which
entries.summary: This command allows edge state styles to be created / modified which will then be usable when creating an [edge state](EdgeStateCommand.md).

entries.name: KnurlingTextCommand
entries.title: Knurling Dimension
entries.summary: This command allows you to create knurling dimensions.

entries.name: KnurlingTextStyleCommand
entries.title: Knurling Dimension Style
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a customized knurling dimension style.

entries.name: LevelRichTextCommand
entries.title: Level note
entries.summary: This command allows to locate altitudes on a part or an assembly.

entries.name: LevelRichTextStyleCommand
entries.title: Level note style
entries.summary: This command allows a customized level note style to be created.

entries.name: ViewOriginCommand
entries.title: View Origin
entries.summary: Several origin symbols can be positioned on the same view.

entries.name: RichTextCommand
entries.title: Rich text
entries.summary: This command allows to create text and the result is directly visible during the entering. Symbols and parameters can be included during the entering. This kind of text is useful to force a too long description to be automatically on 2 lines.

entries.name: SurfaceFinishCommand
entries.title: Surface Finish
entries.summary: This command allows a **Surface finish** symbol to be created in your draft document.

entries.name: TaperRichTextCommand
entries.title: Cone Note
entries.summary: This command allows to create a note displaying the conicity of a conical face.

entries.name: taperrichtextstylecommand
entries.title: Cone Note Style
entries.summary: This command allows to define the style of a [cone note](TaperRichTextCommand.md).

entries.name: TextCommand
entries.title: The **Text** command allows text to be inserted
entries.summary: For a **multi-line text**, use the Ctrl+Entr keys on the keyboard.

entries.name: DatumFeatureCommand
entries.title: Datum Feature
entries.summary: This command allows a tolerance reference symbol to be created.

entries.name: datumtargetcommand
entries.title: Datum target
entries.summary: This command allows to create datum targets for ISO geometric tolerancing according to the ISO 5459 standard.

entries.name: BasicDimensionCommand
entries.title: Basic dimension
entries.summary: - It is possible to hook again an item by double clicking on the dimension line or the attachment line and moving the handle onto another geometry.

entries.name: GeometricToleranceCommand
entries.title: Geometric tolerance
entries.summary: This command allows a geometric tolerance symbol to be created on an item.

entries.name: GeometricToleranceStyleCommand
entries.title: Geometric tolerance style
entries.summary: This command allows geometric tolerance styles to be created / modified which will then be usable with the [Geometric tolerance](GeometricToleranceCommand.md) command.

entries.name: WeldCommand
entries.title: Welding symbol
entries.summary: This command allows a welding **symbol** to be created in your draft document.

entries.name: WeldStyleCommand
entries.title: Welding style
entries.summary: This command allow a customized welding style to be created.

entries.name: ArcLengthDimensionCommand
entries.title: Arc length dimension
entries.summary: This command allows you to dimension the length of an arc.

entries.name: AutomaticDimensionsCommand
entries.title: Automatic Dimensions
entries.summary: This command allows to dimension automatically some dimensions on a view. This command is mainly useful to dimensions extruded bars draft (length, cutting dimensions and angle...)

entries.name: AutomaticCompositeDimensionsCommand
entries.title: Automatic composite dimensions
entries.summary: This command allows to rapidly and automatically dimension a set of drillings with [composites dimensions](../CompositeDimensionCommand.md). Composite dimensions are positioned following 2 directions.

entries.name: bendingsautomaticcompositedimensionscommand
entries.title: Automatic bendings composite dimension
entries.summary: - Only the bendings parallel with the view axes will be taken in consideration.
entries.menu: Cad / Drafting / Dimensions / CompositeDimensions / AutomaticCompositeDimensions / Bends

entries.name: ChangeCompositeDimensionsLineAxisCommand
entries.title: This contextual command allows to permute a dimension on the other side of the view.
entries.summary: This contextual command allows to permute a dimension on the other side of the view.

entries.name: CompositeDimensionCommand
entries.title: Composite dimension
entries.summary: Double clicking on a constituent of a dimension (dimension line or projection line) allows the [handles](javascript:void(0);) to be displayed coupled with reference geometries.

entries.name: CompositeDimensionStyleCommand
entries.title: Composite Dimension Style
entries.summary: This command allows you to create/modify composite dimension styles that can then be use to create a [composite dimension](CompositeDimensionCommand.md).

entries.name: DimensionAsCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to retrieve the style of a dimension in order to create a new dimension.
entries.summary: A dimension created from another dimension retrieves the style and options, which can be modified if necessary. There is no link between the 2 dimensions, so modifying the options of one of the dimensions does not modify the options of the other.

entries.name: DimensionCommand
entries.title: The command **Dimension** allows your draft to
entries.summary: - When creating a dimension between 2 points, it is possible to select a reference direction.

entries.name: DraftAngleDimensionCommand
entries.title: Draft angle dimension
entries.summary: This command allows to dimension a draft.

entries.name: EditDimensionToleranceCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to edit a tolerance to be able to modify it.
entries.summary: This command allows you to edit a tolerance to be able to modify it.

entries.name: HalfPartDimensionCommand
entries.title: Half part dimension
entries.summary: This command allows to dimension symmetric parts (half representation of the part) or revolution parts. It allows as well to dimension of long diameter (when the part is partially represented).

entries.name: SetMaskCommand
entries.title: SetMaskCommand
entries.summary: **The mask** allows to define if an element hide or not the lines behind in order to make the draft comprehension easier. There is 3 masking level: Low, Medium, High.

entries.name: SpatialDimensionCommand
entries.title: 3D Dimension
entries.summary: This command allows you to dimension a perspective view.

entries.name: DimensionsTableCommand
entries.title: Dimensions Table
entries.summary: - For symbols with more than one piece of information, such as roughness symbols, it is possible to select each of the elements separately.

entries.name: DimensionsTableIndexStyleCommand
entries.title: Dimensions Table Index Style
entries.summary: This command allows to create/ modify dimensions table index usable during [dimensions table](DimensionsTableCommand.md) creation.

entries.name: DimensionsTableStyleCommand
entries.title: Dimensions Table Style
entries.summary: This command allows to create/modify [dimensions table](DimensionsTableCommand.md) styles usable during dimensions tables creation.

entries.name: MultipleDraftingsChooseBomCommand
entries.title: This command allows to select or to create a bill of material to use for the multiple drafts.
entries.summary: This command allows to select or to create a bill of material to use for the multiple drafts.

entries.name: MultipleDraftingsCommand
entries.title: Multiple Draftings
entries.summary: This command allows you to draft several documents from an assembly document.

entries.name: MultipleDraftingsMenuCommand
entries.title: Multiple Draftings
entries.summary: This command allows you to draft several documents from a bill of material document. This command is also available from the popup menu of the assembly tab.

entries.name: BorderCommand
entries.title: Frame
entries.summary: This command allows you to modify the format of the border and various options.

entries.name: DraftingFormatCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to modify the format of the drafting document.
entries.summary: This command allows you to modify the format of the drafting document.

entries.name: DrillingsTableCommand
entries.title: Drillings table
entries.summary: - By click the upper line of the title block, the table use the same width than the title block.

entries.name: DrillingsTableIndexStyleCommand
entries.title: Drillings table index style
entries.summary: This command allows to create/ modify drillings table index usable during [drillings table](../DrillingsTableCommand.md) creation.

entries.name: DrillingsTableStyleCommand
entries.title: Drillings table style
entries.summary: This command allows to create/modify drillings table styles usable during drillings tables creation.

entries.name: DrillingsTableStyleEditCommand
entries.title: Drillings table styyle (editing)
entries.summary: This command allows to modify drillings table styles usable during drillings tables creation.

entries.name: ExcelTableCommand
entries.title: Excel Table
entries.summary: - This table can be [exported](../../../../Kernel/UI/D2/Tables/ExportTableCommand.md) in a csv file or text file.

entries.name: DifferenceNoteCommand
entries.title: Difference note
entries.summary: This command allows to create difference note that refers to a line of the [index table](IndexTableCommand.md).

entries.name: DifferenceNoteStyleCommand
entries.title: Difference note style
entries.summary: If a difference note style is modified, the difference notes that are using this style will be automatically updated.

entries.name: IndexTableCommand
entries.title: Index table
entries.summary: - After validating, the table is in edit mode. Use the popup commands or tooltips to complete the table. The table must then be validated by clicking the blue checkmark.

entries.name: IndexTableStyleCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define the style of an index table by choosing columns to include.
entries.summary: This command allows to define the style of an index table by choosing columns to include.

entries.name: coolingstylecommand
entries.title: Cooling Circuit Style
entries.summary: The modification of an cooling attributes style used as the basic style by another style will automatically cause the modification of the later.

entries.name: pinsymbolviewstylecommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create / modify pin symbol style which can be used with the [Pin Symbol](../../../../Mold/UI/Drafting/Views/Pins/pinsymbolviewcommand.md) command.
entries.summary: This command allows you to create / modify pin symbol style which can be used with the [Pin Symbol](../../../../Mold/UI/Drafting/Views/Pins/pinsymbolviewcommand.md) command.

entries.name: BomParameterCommand
entries.title: Parameter from bill of material
entries.summary: This command allows to create a parameter which uses the value of a bill of material cell. For example, if a part is in a draft, and this part is also used in an assembly which has a bill of material document, it is possible to use its index in the draft.

entries.name: importedpropertytextparametercommand
entries.title: Occurrence parameter
entries.summary: This command allows to retrieve a property on the occurrence of a part.

entries.name: ImportedTextParameterCommand
entries.title: Parameter from set
entries.summary: This parameter can be used in the title block for example to retrieve the description value.

entries.name: EditDraftingScaleFactorCommand
entries.title: This command allows the scale factor of the draft document to be modified.
entries.summary: This command allows the scale factor of the draft document to be modified.

entries.name: scheduletablecommand
entries.title: Scenario table
entries.summary: This command allows to display in table form the tasks of a mechanism [scenario](../../../Design/UI/Simulations/Scenarios/ScenarioCommand.md), an exploded scenario or a machining scenario.

entries.name: DivideCellCommand
entries.title: This command allows to divide one or several cell(s) of a title block or sketch.
entries.summary: Constraints are automatically added to lines resulting from the cell division.

entries.name: DraftingSketchCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a sketch into a draft document. In opposition to the [detailing](../Views/Areas/EditDrawingViewAreaCommand.md) that is associated with a view and it scale factor, the sketch of a draft always use a scale factor to 1.
entries.summary: This command allows to create a sketch into a draft document. In opposition to the [detailing](../Views/Areas/EditDrawingViewAreaCommand.md) that is associated with a view and it scale factor, the sketch of a draft always use a scale factor to 1.

entries.name: ImageCommand
entries.title: This command allows an image to be included in a title block.
entries.summary: This command allows an image to be included in a title block.

entries.name: LockSketchModeCommand
entries.title: This mode allows to lock texts or symbols of a sketch to avoid to accidentally move them.
entries.summary: This mode allows to lock texts or symbols of a sketch to avoid to accidentally move them.

entries.name: TableCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a table.
entries.summary: - After validating, the table is in edit mode. Use contextual commands or tooltips to complete the table. The table must then be validated by clicking the blue checkmark.

entries.name: CreateCellMiddleLinesCommand
entries.title: This command allows the middle axes of a cell to be created.
entries.summary: A middle axis is considered as a default construction item and inside the sketch.

entries.name: TitleBlockCommand
entries.title: This command allows a **title block** to be created in your draft document.
entries.summary: The title block is part of the "background" step. This is why the items of the draft (views, dimensions, etc.) are not displayed during editing of a title block.

entries.name: EditDrawingViewAreaCommand
entries.title: This popup command allows you to edit a view in order to complete it with detail (sketch, image, text, ...).
entries.summary: This popup command allows you to edit a view in order to complete it with detail (sketch, image, text, ...).

entries.name: viewareaitemsattributescommand
entries.title: Geometries Attributes
entries.summary: This command allows you to modify the attributes (linetype, width, color, ...) of the view geometries (segments, points).

entries.name: viewareaitemshidingcommand
entries.title: Hide Geometries
entries.summary: This command allows you to mask the geometries of a view.

entries.name: AuxiliaryViewCommand
entries.title: Auxiliary view
entries.summary: The use of the right button on an existing auxiliary view allows:

entries.name: ConvertAuxiliaryViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to convert an auxiliary view to a main view. The link with the main view is then broken. This view will be independent from the other views.
entries.summary: This command allows you to convert an auxiliary view to a main view. The link with the main view is then broken. This view will be independent from the other views.

entries.name: AutomaticAxesCommand
entries.title: Automatic Axes
entries.summary: An axis generated by this command cannot be **deleted**. A right-click deleted on an axis will delete the "Automatic axes" operation and therefore destroy all created axes.

entries.name: AxisCommand
entries.title: The command Axis allows you to create axes in your document.
entries.summary: The selection of a circular profile (fillet, circle, etc.) or a profile from a cylindrical face will automatically cause the creation of the item's middle axis.

entries.name: extendedaxiscommand
entries.title: Resize
entries.summary: The axis resize command create a new axis on the view that replace the original one.

entries.name: SnapCameraCommand
entries.title: This contextual command allows to modify the orientation of the selected view. It will change for the nearest standard orientation (front, left, top,...) of its actual orientation. This command is only available if the view has been created with the unchecked [snap mode](SnapCameraModeCommand.md), and if the part or the assembly has be turned (with the mouse wheel).
entries.summary: This contextual command allows to modify the orientation of the selected view. It will change for the nearest standard orientation (front, left, top,...) of its actual orientation. This command is only available if the view has been created with the unchecked [snap mode](SnapCameraModeCommand.md), and if the part or the assembly has be turned (with the mouse wheel).

entries.name: SnapCameraModeCommand
entries.title: During a dynamic modification of the orientation of the [main view](../MainViewCommand.md), this mode allows the closest standard orientation to be recognized and applied to the view.
entries.summary: This mode is activated by default. When it is deactivated, it is then possible to give an arbitrary orientation to the view.

entries.name: ViewComparisonBuildingCommand
entries.title: This dialog allows to customize the result of [views comparison](ViewComparisonCreationCommand.md).
entries.summary: This dialog allows to customize the result of [views comparison](ViewComparisonCreationCommand.md).

entries.name: ViewComparisonCreationCommand
entries.title: This command allows to compare the content of one view/several views with another minor revision of the same document.
entries.summary: This command allows to compare the content of one view/several views with another minor revision of the same document.

entries.name: StartCroppedViewCommand
entries.title: Cropped View
entries.summary: This command allows you to crop a view by sketch.

entries.name: crosssectionpolygonbuildingcommand
entries.title: This command allows to define the attributes of the cross section polygon.
entries.summary: This command allows to define the attributes of the cross section polygon.

entries.name: CrossSectionPolygonCommand
entries.title: Cross section Polygon
entries.summary: This command allows you to quickly create a cross section polygon. A cross section polygon must be edited.

entries.name: crosssectionpolygonwizardsketchcommand
entries.title: This command allows to create the cross section polygon using the sketch commands.
entries.summary: This command allows to create the cross section polygon using the sketch commands.

entries.name: EditCrossSectionSketchMenuCommand
entries.title: This popup command allows you to edit an existing cross section polygon.
entries.summary: This popup command allows you to edit an existing cross section polygon.

entries.name: StartLocalCrossSectionCommand
entries.title: This command allows a view to be cut locally in order to show an precise zone inside a part or parts.
entries.summary: The reference item defines the depth of the cross section inside the part.

entries.name: StartCrossSectionPolygonCommand
entries.title: Cross Section Polygon
entries.summary: This command allows to create a cross section polygon that can be used during the creation of a cross section in the same document or in another document.

entries.name: DetailSketchStyleCommand
entries.title: This command allows detail sketch styles to be created / modified which
entries.summary: The modification of a dimension style used as the basic style by another style will automatically cause the modification of the later.

entries.name: startdetailsketchcommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a detail sketch that can be used during the creation of a detailed view in the same document or in another document.
entries.summary: This command allows to create a detail sketch that can be used during the creation of a detailed view in the same document or in another document.

entries.name: StartDetailViewCommand
entries.title: StartDetailViewCommand
entries.summary: - You can easily move the frame of the detail view by selecting and dragging it.

entries.name: DirectionSketchStyleCommand
entries.title: Direction Sketch Style
entries.summary: The modification of a dimension style used as the basic style by another style will automatically cause the modification of the later.

entries.name: startdirectionsketchcommand
entries.title: Direction Sketch
entries.summary: This command allows to create a direction sketch that can be used during the creation of a [view along a direction](StartDirectionViewCommand.md) in the same document or in another document.

entries.name: StartDirectionViewCommand
entries.title: View along a direction
entries.summary: By right clicking on an existing view along a direction you can:

entries.name: AutomaticExceptionCommand
entries.title: Automatic exception
entries.summary: - The exception can be done for one element only (part or sub-assembly). - Once an automatic exception has been applied to a view, it is no longer possible to apply a [manual exception](ExceptionCommand.md) (and vice versa).

entries.name: ExceptionCommand
entries.title: This command allows to personalize the contents of a view by applying
entries.summary: This command allows to personalize the contents of a view by applying exceptions (lines attributes, unsectionability,...) to entities of this view. The selected parts use parameters defined in the exception. For example, if the lines are black and if the exception of the part A indicates green, part A lines in the view will be green.

entries.name: ExceptionStyleCommand
entries.title: Exception style
entries.summary: This style allows to personalize the contents of a view by applying exceptions (lines attributes, unsectionability,...) to entities of this view. The selected parts use parameters defined in the exception. For example, if the lines are black and the part An exception indicates green, part A lines in the view will be green.

entries.name: ExceptionStyleEditCommand
entries.title: This style allows to personalize the contents of a view by applying
entries.summary: This style allows to personalize the contents of a view by applying exceptions (lines attributes, unsectionability,...) to entities of this view. The selected parts use parameters defined in the exception. For example, if the lines are black and the part An exception indicates green, part A lines in the view will be green.

entries.name: fiberssymbolstylecommand
entries.title: Fibers symbol style
entries.summary: This command allows a customized fibers symbol style to be created.

entries.name: fiberssymbolviewcommand
entries.title: Fibers symbol
entries.summary: This command allows to add the [fibers](../../../../Design/UI/Analysis/PartFibersCommand.md) symbol on a view.

entries.name: AutomaticHatchingCommand
entries.title: Automatic Hatching
entries.summary: When we click on hatching area in a view, the area is automatically added in the option Borders. It can be easily to define the borders value. It is more visual.

entries.name: CopyHatchingCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to copy the hatching of a part in order to apply it in other sections. During this copy, some modifications (spacing, angle, type of hatchings, ...) can be done to copied hatchings.
entries.summary: This command allows you to copy the hatching of a part in order to apply it in other sections. During this copy, some modifications (spacing, angle, type of hatchings, ...) can be done to copied hatchings.

entries.name: HatchingCommand
entries.title: Edit Hatching Lines
entries.summary: By default, hatches are defined regarding the [material](../../../../Design/UI/Materials/MaterialAndCoatingCommand.md) of the part.

entries.name: StartInterruptedViewCommand
entries.title: Interrupted View
entries.summary: - When selecting the reference view, a connection direction is automatically proposed. This corresponds to the longest axis in the view.

entries.name: KinematicsgraphCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a kinematics graph in a draft. This graph displays existing rigid groups and joints like a scheme.
entries.summary: This command allows to create a kinematics graph in a draft. This graph displays existing rigid groups and joints like a scheme.

entries.name: KinematicsGraphResetPositionCommand
entries.title: This contextual command allows to restore initial graph boxes positions.
entries.summary: This contextual command allows to restore initial graph boxes positions.

entries.name: KinematicsGraphStyleCommand
entries.title: Kinematics graph style
entries.summary: This command allows to create / modify a kinematics graph style which can be used when creating a [kinematics graph](KinematicsgraphCommand.md).

entries.name: AlignMainViewsCommand
entries.title: Alignment of Views
entries.summary: - The alignment is done regarding the center point of views or the 3d reference points if they have been used in the projection view options.

entries.name: LayoutSketchCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create different rectangles. They will be used to position hooked views. Each view has to be positioned in a rectangle by using the [Optimize view layout](OptimizeViewLayoutCommand.md) command.
entries.summary: This command allows to create different rectangles. They will be used to position hooked views. Each view has to be positioned in a rectangle by using the [Optimize view layout](OptimizeViewLayoutCommand.md) command.

entries.name: OptimizeViewLayoutCommand
entries.title: This function optimizes the space available on the plan.
entries.summary: This command is useful for a [template](../../../../../Pdm/UI/Items/Overview.md#Template) with empty views when using it to create a new draft or multiple drafts.

entries.name: PositionConstraintCommand
entries.title: This command allows relative position constraints to be added between views.
entries.summary: To update your plane after adding constraints, use the command [Optimize views layout](OptimizeViewLayoutCommand.md).

entries.name: ViewLayoutProfileCommand
entries.title: This command allows to hook a view to one of [layout sketch](LayoutSketchCommand.md) rectangles, to be able to [optimize the view layout](OptimizeViewLayoutCommand.md) of a draft.
entries.summary: This command allows to hook a view to one of [layout sketch](LayoutSketchCommand.md) rectangles, to be able to [optimize the view layout](OptimizeViewLayoutCommand.md) of a draft.

entries.name: ViewMagneticBorderOptionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define the position of a virtual border where the [automatic bill of material indexes](../../Bom/AutomaticBomRichTextCommand.md) will be automatically positioned. The entered value is the distance between this border and the enclosing box of the view.
entries.summary: This command allows to define the position of a virtual border where the [automatic bill of material indexes](../../Bom/AutomaticBomRichTextCommand.md) will be automatically positioned. The entered value is the distance between this border and the enclosing box of the view.

entries.name: MainViewCommand
entries.title: Main view
entries.summary: A conical view has some limitations. It can only be cropped or used by a detail view (Cross section view, interrupted view, ...) are not allowed. Also, it is not possible to dimension it.

entries.name: ExactProjectionCommand
entries.title: This button allows you to turn on the **exact projection** mode.
entries.summary: - If a view is **incomplete**, change to **exact projection** mode.

entries.name: HatchingOptionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to define globaly the hatching parameters (type, spacing, orientation, color and layer) of the material categories.
entries.summary: The parameters defined using this command have low level priority, they can be modified using the other hatching modification commands (automatic hatching, hatching edition,...).

entries.name: PerspectiveTypeOptionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define a perspective type.It can be inherited for the standard or can be forced as **Dimetric** or as **Isometric**.
entries.summary: This command allows to define a perspective type.It can be inherited for the standard or can be forced as **Dimetric** or as **Isometric**.

entries.name: ProjectionTypeOptionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define the projection type. It can be inherited or forced to ISO or ANSI.
entries.summary: This command allows to define the projection type. It can be inherited or forced to ISO or ANSI.

entries.name: RefreshingModeCommand
entries.title: Auto Refresh
entries.summary: This mode allows to automatically update or not, each modification of the draft.

entries.name: RefreshingModeOptionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define the views refreshing mode.(this option is only available for the [Exact projection](ExactProjectionCommand.md) mode).
entries.summary: This command allows to define the views refreshing mode.(this option is only available for the [Exact projection](ExactProjectionCommand.md) mode).

entries.name: ScaleFactorOptionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows to activate automatic scaling of projected sets.
entries.summary: This command allows to activate automatic scaling of projected sets.

entries.name: ViewIndexModeOptionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define how are managed the views index. If these indexes are general in a draft, each view index will be different (for example, the detail view will be B, the cross section C-C). If the view index is done by type of view, a detail view A and a cross section A-A can exist in the draft.
entries.summary: This command allows to define how are managed the views index. If these indexes are general in a draft, each view index will be different (for example, the detail view will be B, the cross section C-C). If the view index is done by type of view, a detail view A and a cross section A-A can exist in the draft.

entries.name: ViewOriginCommand
entries.title: This contextual command allows to define the view origin representation displayed when creating a [drillings table](../../Drillings/DrillingsTableCommand.md).

entries.name: ViewOriginStyleCommand
entries.title: View origin style
entries.summary: This command allows to create a customized style of the view origin representation.

entries.name: ViewPlaneCommand
entries.title: View Plane
entries.summary: In a Draft document, this command allows you to create a plane for positioning a [3D Dimension](../../Dimensions/SpatialDimensions/SpatialDimensionCommand.md).

entries.name: PlanningMainViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows to display in strong lines the elements located
entries.summary: This command allows to display in strong lines the elements located under a plane and to display in dotted lines those located above.

entries.name: ProjectedAnnotationsCommand
entries.title: Projected Annotations
entries.summary: This command allows you to project annotations (dimensions, tolerance references, tolerances, ...) in a view of the template document.

entries.name: ProjectedScreenSizeTextsCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to get the screen texts of the 3d document into a view of the drafting document.
entries.summary: This command allows you to get the screen texts of the 3d document into a view of the drafting document.

entries.name: ProjectionModeCommand
entries.title: This command allows the projection mode of your draft document to be chosen.
entries.summary: This command allows the projection mode of your draft document to be chosen.

entries.name: DisplayOffsetTypeCommand
entries.title: Display Priority
entries.summary: In a view, when several shapes are superposed, this command allows you to set one (or several) of them on foreground and the others one on background.

entries.name: EditViewProjectionSetCommand
entries.title: Edit Set
entries.summary: This command allows you to define sets which will be referenced in the draft.

entries.name: MainProjectionSetCommand
entries.title: Main Set
entries.summary: This command allows you to define the main set of the draft document.

entries.name: ProjectionSetCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to define sets which will be referenced in the
entries.summary: In the case the source document is an **electrode document**, the options of this section are different.

entries.name: ProjectionSetOccurrenceCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a set from a projection set defined in another document.

entries.name: ViewStyleCommand
entries.title: View Style
entries.summary: - The modification of a view style used as a basic style by another style will automatically cause the modification of the latter.

entries.name: ViewStyleDoubleClickCommand
entries.title: View Style
entries.summary: - The modification of a view style used as a basic style by another style will automatically cause the modification of the latter.

entries.name: RemoveTrimmingVolumeCommand
entries.title: This contextual command allows to remove a [trimming volume](TrimmingVolumeCommand.md).
entries.summary: This contextual command allows to remove a [trimming volume](TrimmingVolumeCommand.md).

entries.name: TrimmingVolumeCommand
entries.title: This command allows to use a trimming volume in a view.
entries.summary: The defined trimming volume is linked to the [set to project](../ProjectionSets/ProjectionSetCommand.md) which is used in the selected view.
